chapter_context.summary: All the tribes of Israel anoint David king at Hebron; David captures the stronghold of Zion (Jerusalem), making it his capital; David defeats the Philistines twice.

outline.text: David Anointed King Over All Israel at Hebron
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Anointed King Over All Israel at Hebron (5:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: David Captures Zion (Jerusalem) from the Jebusites
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Captures Zion (Jerusalem) from the Jebusites (5:6-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Hiram of Tyre Sends Materials; David's Prosperity and Family
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hiram of Tyre Sends Materials; David's Prosperity and Family (5:11-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: David Inquires of God; Defeats the Philistines Twice
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Inquires of God; Defeats the Philistines Twice (5:17-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

key_themes.title: The Davidic Covenant
key_themes.description: God's unconditional promise of an eternal dynasty, ultimately pointing forward to the Messiah.

key_themes.title: Sin and Consequences
key_themes.description: While God offers complete forgiveness for sincere repentance, the earthly consequences of sin remain.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 5 demonstrate the grace of God in the midst of human failure?
What lessons about leadership and accountability can you apply to your own life from this chapter?

cultural_historical.title: Jerusalem as Capital
cultural_historical.text: By capturing Jerusalem, a neutral city on the border of Judah and Benjamin, David strategically united the fractured northern and southern tribes under a single capital.
